product_overview: A retro-futuristic, web-based simulated UNIX terminal that serves as an interactive developer/ security portfolio. Users navigate and discover projects, skills, and contact links by typing commands, using tab-completion and keyboard history in a single-page interface served at '/'.
core_goals: Present portfolio content through an immersive, keyboard-first terminal UI to demonstrate technical and security skills.
Provide accessible alternatives (clickable commands / GUI escape hatch) while preserving the terminal illusion.
Defend against common client-side threats (XSS) and show security-conscious engineering practices in the UI and code.
Expose projects, skills, and contact information via realistic terminal commands and readable outputs.
Support fast, discoverable navigation with Tab auto-completion, command history, and predictable error messaging.

features.name: Main Terminal (Home)
features.description: Central CLI prompt and command buffer where users type commands (help, whoami, clear, man, etc.), see outputs printed, and use keyboard interactions.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See blinking cursor at guest@sec-eng:~$ -> Type help -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing available commands and short descriptions -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
Navigate to / -> Type whoami -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing user identity and brief bio -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
Navigate to / -> Type <script>alert(1)</script> -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing escaped/sanitized input and a security warning message (no alert popup)
Navigate to / -> Type clear -> Press Enter -> See terminal buffer cleared and prompt remains at bottom

features.name: Projects (ls ./projects and cat project files)
features.description: Directory-style listing of portfolio projects via 'ls ./projects' and detailed write-ups via 'cat' of project files (e.g., projects/portfolio.txt, projects/vps.txt).
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type ls ./projects -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing tabulated project file list with permissions and executable markers -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed

Navigate to / -> Type cat projects/portfolio.txt -> Press Enter -> View command outputs printing the portfolio project write-up including stack and GitHub link -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
Navigate to / -> Type cat projects/nonexistent.txt -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing error: bash: ./project: No such file or directory (red error text)
Navigate to / -> Type ./project_name.sh -> Press Enter -> View command outputs executing simulated demo or printing project details (if project marked +x) -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed

features.name: Skills (top -u skills)
features.description: A process-monitor style view showing languages, tools and proficiency levels with ASCII bar graphs and dynamic numbers to highlight technical strengths.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type top -u skills -> Press Enter -> View command outputs rendering skill table with PID/USER/%CPU bars and fluctuating values -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
Navigate to / -> Type top -u skills -> Press Enter -> View skill view -> Press q -> See terminal prompt restored at guest@sec-eng:~$
Navigate to / -> Type top --unknownflag -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing usage/help error message and suggested valid flags (red or warning text)

features.name: Contact (traceroute me)
features.description: Network-style traceroute that prints sequential hops and resolves to clickable contact endpoints (GitHub, LinkedIn, mailto).
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type traceroute me -> Press Enter -> View command outputs printing sequential hops (one line per hop) -> Final hop prints contact URLs (github, linkedin, mailto) -> Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
Navigate to / -> Type traceroute me -> Press Enter -> View contact URLs -> Click github.com/username -> Browser opens new tab to GitHub
Navigate to / -> Type traceroute unknown-host -> Press Enter -> View command outputs showing 'host unreachable' or 'no route to host' error message

features.name: Boot Sequence
features.description: Initial simulated hardware boot overlay with rapid syslog-like output that transitions into the Main Terminal; click-to-skip or auto-advance to the prompt.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See rapid kernel log lines and mounting messages printing top-to-bottom -> Wait until boot finishes -> See login prompt auto-type guest and then land at the main terminal prompt
Navigate to / -> Click anywhere during boot -> Boot sequence skips immediately -> See main terminal prompt at guest@sec-eng:~$
Navigate to / on a constrained device -> Observe boot fast-forwarding or offer skip if performance threshold exceeded -> Click to accept fast-forward -> See main terminal prompt

features.name: Input Assistance (Tab auto-completion & Command History)
features.description: Keyboard helpers: Tab completes partial commands or filenames; Up/Down arrows navigate previous commands; Enter executes current input. Supports cycling/completion and shows available matches when ambiguous.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type pr -> Use Tab auto-completion -> See input auto-complete to projects -> Press Enter -> Execute projects-related command (e.g., ls ./projects) -> View command outputs

Navigate to / -> Type ls ./projects -> Press Enter -> Type another command -> Use Up arrow to navigate to previous commands -> See previous command populated in input -> Press Enter -> Re-execute previous command
Navigate to / -> Type c -> Use Tab auto-completion -> See multiple possible completions displayed (contact, cat) -> Input remains unchanged until user disambiguates

Navigate to / -> Type a-very-long-command-name-exceeding-limit -> Press Enter -> View command outputs handling long input (either executed or showing validation/truncation notice) and verify no UI crash

features.name: Terminal Portfolio Core
features.description: A web-based terminal interface acting as a portfolio
user_interactions: Type commands like help, ls, whoami, clear, cat, contact, socials
Use Tab auto-completion for commands and project files
Use up/down arrows for command history navigation
Press Enter to execute a command empty input or long input
Execute cat projects/vps.txt and cat projects/portfolio.txt
View command outputs in the terminal display
Verify auto-scroll to bottom of the feed
